examples: Controlling Emissions by Queue Relocation, Malcolm Neil and Pete Sykes. JCT Signals Symposium, September 2008.
The Joint Committee on Taxation (JCT) is the official scorekeeper for all tax legislation passed by the Congress.
Its staff writes reports on various tax issues, often in collaboration with JCT, which calculates any revenue estimates.
The JCT estimates the revenue effect of all tax proposals and it prepares an annual compendium of tax expenditures.
